name: Intercom API
what_it_does: Intercom's REST API for managing customer conversations, contacts, companies, and automated messaging workflows. Enables agents to create and reply to support conversations, manage contact and company records, trigger message sequences, and build AI-powered support bots integrated with Intercom's messaging platform.
use_cases: Creating and replying to customer conversations from automated agent workflows
Managing contact and company data for CRM automation
Creating and routing support tickets via programmatic rules
Triggering automated message sequences based on agent-detected user behavior
Building AI-powered customer support bots integrated into the Intercom inbox
not_for: Teams not using Intercom (no value without the Intercom platform)
Simple email-only support where a full platform is overkill
Teams on tight budgets (Intercom is among the most expensive customer support platforms)
Real-time high-volume event-driven messaging at scale
best_when: An agent needs to read, respond to, or route customer conversations in Intercom, or sync contact/company data for customer success workflows.
avoid_when: You are not using Intercom, or need a simple helpdesk without Intercom's in-app messaging and lifecycle capabilities.

auth.notes: Access tokens for single-workspace integrations. OAuth 2.0 for multi-workspace apps with scope-based permissions. Token-based auth is simplest for agents. Tokens are workspace-scoped. API versioning is mandatory via Intercom-Version request header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 14-day free trial; Startup program offers free access for qualifying early-stage companies
pricing.notes: One of the more expensive customer support platforms. Most businesses need at minimum the Essential plan ($39/seat/mo) for full API access. Startup program provides free access for qualifying companies.

performance.rate_limits: 1,000 requests/minute per workspace; some endpoints have lower limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include type, message, and errors array with field-level detail. API versioning is explicit via header, which helps maintain compatibility. Error codes are well-documented in the reference. Generally agent-friendly error format.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Conversation creation and replies are not idempotent on retry. Agents must implement external state tracking to avoid duplicate messages.
known_agent_gotchas: API versioning is required via Intercom-Version header — must specify correct version or responses will be unexpected
Replying to a conversation requires correct author type (bot vs admin) — bots need specific Intercom app setup to reply as a bot
Conversations vs tickets have different data models — check which is in use for your workspace before writing code
Contact deduplication by email is not automatic — agents can easily create duplicate contacts without checking first
Webhook signatures must be validated using the client secret to prevent spoofed event ingestion
Rate limits are per workspace, not per token — other integrations on the same workspace consume the shared quota

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with workspace-scoped tokens. Single-workspace access tokens are straightforward for agent auth. Webhook signature verification available. EU and US data residency options satisfy GDPR requirements.

score_rationale: Intercom API is reasonably agent-friendly with good documentation, cursor pagination, and clear error formats. The main friction points are the absence of idempotency keys, mandatory API version header, and the high cost of the platform itself. Bot reply setup requires specific Intercom app configuration that can block agent deployment. Best suited for teams already invested in the Intercom ecosystem.
